figures.caption: Figure 4: Channel ∆T10: x–y slices of the streamwise velocity at z = 8 for the fDNS reference, FNO, IFactFormer, and Ms-MoE-IFactFormer (top to bottom). The columns correspond to rollout steps n = 500, 1000, 2000, and 4000.

Figure 5: Channel ∆T10: long-time-averaged wall-normal statistics for the fDNS reference, DSM, WALE, IFactFormer, and Ms-MoE-IFactFormer. Panels (a)–(e) show ⟨u+⟩, ⟨u′v′⟩, u+rms, v+rms, and w+rms, respectively.
Figure 9: HIT ∆T10: kinetic energy spectra E(k) for the fDNS reference, DSM, FNO, IFactFormer, and Ms-MoE-IFactFormer. Panels (a)–(d) correspond to t/τ ≈ 4, 8, 16, and 32, respectively.

Figure 10: HIT ∆T10: PDFs of the normalized longitudinal velocity increment δru/urms at r = ∆ for the fDNS reference, DSM, FNO, IFactFormer, and Ms-MoE-IFactFormer. Panels (a)–(d) correspond to t/τ ≈ 4, 8, 16, and 32, respectively.

Figure 2: Channel ∆T50: x–y slices of the streamwise velocity at z = 8 for the fDNS reference, FNO, IFactFormer, and Ms-MoE-IFactFormer (top to bottom). The columns correspond to rollout steps n = 100, 500, 1000, and 2000.

Figure 3: Channel ∆T50: long-time-averaged wall-normal statistics for the fDNS reference, DSM, WALE, IFactFormer, and Ms-MoE-IFactFormer. Panels (a)–(e) show ⟨u+⟩, ⟨u′v′⟩, u+rms, v+rms, and w+rms, respectively.

Figure 8: HIT ∆T50: PDFs of the normalized vorticity magnitude ω̄/ω̄rms fDNS for the fDNS reference, DSM, FNO, IFactFormer, and Ms-MoEIFactFormer. Legends mark unstable FNO rollouts as FNO (NaN). Panels (a)–(d) correspond to t/τ ≈ 10, 20, 40, and 80, respectively.

Figure A.12: Schematic overview of IFactFormer-m in latent space. The left panel illustrates the latent residual update with positional encoding, and the right panel shows the factorized-attention block used inside the update.

Figure 6: HIT ∆T50: kinetic energy spectra E(k) for the fDNS reference, DSM, FNO, IFactFormer, and Ms-MoE-IFactFormer. Legends mark unstable FNO rollouts as FNO (NaN). Panels (a)–(d) correspond to t/τ ≈ 10, 20, 40, and 80, respectively.

Figure 7: HIT ∆T50: PDFs of the normalized longitudinal velocity increment δru/urms at r = ∆ for the fDNS reference, DSM, FNO, IFactFormer, and Ms-MoE-IFactFormer. Legends mark unstable FNO rollouts as FNO (NaN). Panels (a)–(d) correspond to t/τ ≈ 10, 20, 40, and 80, respectively.
Table 2: Model configurations at different sampling intervals.
Figure 1: Ms-MoE-IFactFormer framework with a shared expert, scale-routed experts, and a stride-indexed multilayer perceptron (MLP) corrector selected by the queried stride s.

Figure 11: HIT ∆T10: PDFs of the normalized vorticity magnitude ω̄/ω̄rms fDNS for the fDNS reference, DSM, FNO, IFactFormer, and Ms-MoEIFactFormer. Panels (a)–(d) correspond to t/τ ≈ 4, 8, 16, and 32, respectively.
